title: Solomon Ben Judah Gabirol (Died Between 1070-80.) (Translations From The Hebrew Poets Of Medaeval Spain.)
content:
"Am I sipping the honey of the lips?
    Am I drunk with the wine of a kiss?
    Have I culled the flowers of the cheek,
    Have I sucked the fresh fragrance of the breath?
    Nay, it is the Song of Gabirol that has revived me,
    The perfume of his youthful, spring-tide breeze."
     - MOSES BEN ESRA.

    "I will engrave my songs indelibly upon the heart of
    the world, so that no one can efface them."
     - GABIROL.
meaning:
Emma Lazarus's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Solomon Ben Judah Gabirol (Died Between 1070-80.) (Translations From The Hebrew Poets Of Medaeval Spain.)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
